verses.sanskrit: अवष्टब्ध धनुम् रामम् क्रुद्धम् च रिपु घातिनम् ।ददर्श आश्रमम् आगम्य खरः सह पुरःसरैः ॥३-२५-१॥
verses.text:
Khara on arriving at the hermitage along with forerunning soldiers saw the enemy-eliminator and wrathful 
Rama holding fast to his bow. [3-25-1]

verses.sanskrit: तम् दृष्ट्वा सगुणम् चापम् उद्यम्य खर निःस्वनम् ।रामस्य अभिमुखम् सूतम् चोद्यताम् इति अचोदयत् ॥३-२५-२॥
verses.text:
On seeing Rama that bawling Khara raised his string-taut bow that makes an ass-like braying twang, drove 
his charioteer crazy saying, "drive to the fore of that Rama..." [3-25-2]

verses.sanskrit: स खरस्य आज्ञया सूतः तुरगान् समचोदयत् ।यत्र रामो महाबाहुः एको धुन्वन् धनुः स्थितः ॥३-२५-३॥
verses.text:
On the orders of Khara the charioteer drove the horses to there where the mighty armed Rama is standing 
single-handedly while swaying his bow. [3-25-3]

verses.sanskrit: तम् तु निष्पतितम् दृष्ट्वा सर्वे ते रजनी चराः ।मुंचमाना महानादम् सचिवाः पर्यवारयन् ॥३-२५-४॥
verses.text:
On seeing Khara descending on Rama then all those nightwalkers who are the deputies of Khara encircled 
Khara venting out loud battle cries. [3-25-4]

verses.sanskrit: स तेषाम् यातुधानानाम् मध्ये रथः गतः खरः ।बभूव मध्ये ताराणाम् लोहितांग इव उदितः ॥३-२५-५॥
verses.text:
Khara staying on his chariot in the thick of those demons appeared to be like coppery-red planet Mars 
up-shot in the thick of stars. [3-25-5]

verses.sanskrit: ततः शर सहस्रेन रामम् अप्रतिम ओजसम् ।अर्दयित्वाअ महानादम् ननाद समरे खरः ॥३-२५-६॥
verses.text:
In his proneness to war then Khara assaulted that uniquely forceful Rama with a thousand arrows and 
blared a roaring battle cry. [3-25-6]

verses.sanskrit: ततः तम् भीम धन्वानम् क्रुद्धाः सर्वे निशाचराः ।रामम् नाना विधैः शस्त्रैः अभ्यवर्षन्त दुर्जयम् ॥३-२५-७॥
verses.text:
All of those infuriated nightwalkers then incessantly rained diverse weapons on that invincibleRama 
who is handling a horrific bow. [3-25-7]

verses.sanskrit: मुद्गरैः आयसैः शूलैः प्रासैः खड्गैः परश्वधैः ।राक्षसाः समरे रामम् निजघ्नू रोष तत्पराः ॥३-२५-८॥
verses.text:
Those demons that are already caught up with rancour bombarded Rama with bludgeons, lances, tridents, 
darts and hatchets. [3-25-8]

verses.sanskrit: ते वलाहक संकाशा महाकाया महाबलाः ।अभ्यधावन्त काकुत्स्थम् रथैः वाजिभिः एव च ॥३-२५-९॥
verses.text:
Those massive bodied and exceptionally mighty demons came jostling like immense clouds in their eagerness 
to kill Rama in that war, while some mounted on chariots and horsebacks, and some more riding mountain-ridges 
like elephants rushed in on Rama. [3-25-9,10a]

verses.sanskrit: गजैः पर्वत कूट अभैः रामम् युद्धे जिंघासवः ।ते रामे शर वर्षाणि व्यसृजन् रक्षसाम् गणाः ॥३-२५-१०॥
verses.text:
And those bands of demons have outpoured arrows on Rama, as with enormous black-clouds disemboguing 
torrents on a lofty mountain. [3-25-10b, 11a]

verses.sanskrit: शैलेन्द्रम् इव धाराभिर् वर्षमाणा महाधनाः ।सर्वैः परिवृतो रामो राक्षसैः कॄरदर्शिनैः ॥३-२५-११॥
verses.text:
When hemmed in with those grisly-looking demons Rama appeared like Rudra surrounded by his divine retinue 
called prathama gaNa -s, on certain days, whereupon he starts his all-destructive 
cosmic-dance. [3-25-11b, 12a]

verses.sanskrit: तिथिषु इव महादेवो वृतः पारिषदाम् गणैः ।तानि मुक्तानि शस्त्राणि यातुधानैः स राघवः ॥३-२५-१२॥
verses.text:
That Raghava has pre-empted those arrows discharged by the demons with his excruciatingly pointed arrows 
as an ocean would do with the river's onrush. [3-25-12b, 13a]

verses.sanskrit: प्रतिजग्राह विशिखैः नदि ओघान् इव सागरः ।स तैः प्रहरणैः घोरैः भिन्न गात्रो न विव्यथे ॥३-२५-१३॥
verses.text:
Even if his body is gashed with those gruesome assault weapons Rama is not enfeebled, as with the enormous 
Mt. Meru that can withstand even if battered by very many highly blazing thunderbolts of Indra. [3-25-13, 
14a]

verses.sanskrit: रामः प्रदीप्तैर् बहुभिर् वज्रैर् इव महा अचलः ।स विद्धः क्षतज दिग्धः सर्व गात्रेषु राघवः ॥३-२५-१४॥
verses.text:
Bruised and bedaubed with blood on all his limbs that Rama of Raghu's dynasty took shape of the sun 
enshrouded by cloudscapes at eventide. [3-25-14b, 15a]

verses.sanskrit: बभूव रामः सन्ध्य अभ्रैः दिवाकर इव आवृतः ।विषेदुर् देव गन्धर्वाः सिद्धाः च परम ऋषयः ॥३-२५-१५॥
verses.text:
On seeing lone Rama beset by many demons, then the gods, celestials, saints and sublime-sages are dispirited. 
[3-25-15b, 16a]

verses.sanskrit: एकम् सहस्रैः बहुभिः तदा दृष्ट्वा समावृतम् ।ततो रामः तु सुसंक्रुद्धो मण्डली कृत कार्मुकः ॥३-२५-१६॥
verses.text:
But Rama on becoming highly infuriated curved his bow to a full circularity on stretching the bowstring 
up to his ear, and then jetted out trenchant arrows in hundreds. Why hundreds? He surged them in thousands. 
[3-25-16b, 17a]

verses.sanskrit: ससर्ज निशितान् बाणान् शतशः अथ सहस्रशः ।दुरवारान् दुर्विषहान् कालपाश उपमान् रणे ॥३-२५-१७॥
verses.text:
In that war Rama sportily discharged eagle-feathered arrows adorned in gold which are unstoppable and 
intolerable, and which in simile are the leads of Time-god. [3-25-17b, 18a]

verses.sanskrit: मुमोच लीलया रामः कंकपत्रान् कांचन भूषणान् ।ते शराः शत्रु सैन्येषु मुक्ता रामेण लीलया ॥३-२५-१८॥
verses.text:
Those arrows that are playfully discharged on the army of enemy have grabbed the lives of demons like 
the tethers flung by the Time-god. [3-25-18b, 19a]

verses.sanskrit: आददू रक्षसाम् प्राणान् पाशाः कालकृता इव ।भित्त्वा राक्षस देहान् ताम् ते शरा रुधिर आप्लुताः ॥३-२५-१९॥
verses.text:
On impaling the bodies demons, the arrows swamped in blood have gone into welkin, where they are aglow 
with flares equalling inflamed flames. [3-25-19b, 20a]

verses.sanskrit: अंतरिक्ष गता रेजुः दीप्त अग्नि सम तेजसः ।असंख्येयाः तु रामस्य सायकाः चाप मण्डलात् ॥३-२५-२०॥
verses.text:
Innumerable and highly frightening arrows fusilladed from the nimbus of the bow of Rama that resulted 
as the filchers of the lives of demons. [3-25-20b, 21a]

verses.sanskrit: विनिष्पेतुः अतीव उग्रा रक्षः प्राण अपहारिणः ।तैः धनूंषि ध्वज अग्राणि चर्माणि च शिरांसि च ॥३-२५-२१॥
verses.text:
In that war, shooting now with hundreds, and now with thousands of such arrows, Rama shredded those 
many bows, pinnacles of war-flags, armours and the like. And even shred are the heads of demons, along 
with their ornamented arms and thighs similar to the trunks of elephants. [3-25-21b, 22]

verses.sanskrit: बहून् स हस्त आभरणान् ऊरून् करि कर उपमान् ।चिछेद रामः समरे शतशः अथ सहस्रशः ॥३-२५-२२॥
हयान् कांचन सन्नाहान् रथ युक्तान् स सारथीन् ।गजाम् च स गज आरोहान् स हयान् सारधिनः तदा ॥३-२५-२३॥
verses.text:
With the arrows flung from the bow-bowstring-nimbus of Rama, he indeed shredded and scraped the horses 
hitched with golden harnesses, and the chariots to which they are yoked, and even their charioteers. 
Likewise, he even ripped off the elephants along with their riders, and the horses along with their 
cavaliers. And on eliminating infantrymen in that war Rama led them to the abode of Yama, the Time-god. 
[3-25-23, 24] [3-25-23]

verses.sanskrit: चिछिदुः बिभिदुः च एव राम बाणा गुण च्युताः ।पदातीन् समरे हत्वा हि अनयत् यम सदनम् ॥३-२५-२४॥
ततो नालीक नाराचैः तीक्ष्ण अग्रैः विकर्णिभिः ।भीमम् आर्त स्वरम् चक्रुः छिद्यमाना निशाचराः ॥३-२५-२५॥
verses.text:
While ripped to pieces with tubular arrows, with arrows made of iron, and with sharp-edged and crescent-tipped 
arrows those demons have let out disgusting cries of anguish. [3-25-25]

verses.sanskrit: तत् सैन्यम् निशितैः बाणैः अर्दितम् मर्म भेदिभिः ।न रामेण  सुखम् लेभे शुष्कम् वनम् इव अग्निना ॥३-२५-२६॥
verses.text:
When those arrows that gore the critical parts are thus harrowing, that army is discomposed like a forest 
burnt to a cinder by wild-fire. [3-25-26]

verses.sanskrit: केचिद् भीम बलाः शूराः प्रासान् शूलान् परश्वधान् ।चिक्षिपुः परम क्रुद्धा रामाय रजनीचराः ॥३-२५-२७॥
verses.text:
Some of the grotesquely sinewy and braving nightwalkers are highly enraged and dashed their darts, tridents, 
and hatchets towards Rama. [3-25-27]

verses.sanskrit: तेषाम् बाणैः महाबाहुः शस्त्राणि आवार्य वीर्यवान् ।जहार समरे प्राणान् चिच्छेद च शिरो धरान् ॥३-२५-२८॥
verses.text:
On fending off those weapons with his arrows that mighty armed and valorous Rama filched away their 
lives by shearing off their necks in that war. [3-25-28]

verses.sanskrit: ते छिन्न शिरसः पेतुः छिन्न चर्म शरासनाः ।सुपर्ण वात विक्षिप्ता जगत्याम् पादपा यथा ॥३-२५-२९॥
verses.text:
Those demons crashed down to earth with their heads severed and shields and bows wrecked, like the trees 
strewn around on earth owing to the gust raised by Garuda, the Divine Eagle, when he takes a sudden 
flight in heavens. [3-25-29]

verses.sanskrit: अवशिष्टाः च ये तत्र विषण्णाः ते निशाचराः ।खरम् एव अभ्यधावन्त शरणार्थम् शर आहताः ॥३-२५-३०॥
verses.text:
Those nightwalkers that are battered by those arrows and remaining there, they desperately rushed towards 
Khara alone seeking shelter. [3-25-30]

verses.sanskrit: तान् सर्वान् धनुर् आदाय समाश्वास्य च दूषणः ।अभ्यधावत सुसंक्रुद्धः क्रुद्धः [रुद्रम्] क्रुद्ध इव अन्तकः ॥३-२५-३१॥
verses.text:
Comforting all of them and taking his bow, very highly infuriated Duushana rushed towards that infuriated 
Rama like the furious Eliminator Yama. That highly infuriated Duushana rushed towards Rama as highly infuriated Yama rushes towards Rudra, 
where Rudra being the ultimate eliminator himself, angry rush of Yama towards Rudra is therefore futile. 
[3-25-31]

verses.sanskrit: निवृत्ताः तु पुनः सर्वे दूषण आश्रय निर्भयाः ।रामम् एव अभ्यधावन्त साल ताल शिल आयुधाः ॥३-२५-३२॥
verses.text:
Coming under the shelter of Duushana all of those demons are but emboldened and returned, and they once 
again rushed towards Rama with saala trees, palm trees and boulders as their weapons. [3-25-32]

verses.sanskrit: शूल मुद्गर हस्ताः च पाश हस्ता महाबलाः ।सृजन्तः शर वर्षाणि शस्त्र वर्षाणि संयुगे॥३-२५-३३॥
verses.text:
Handling tridents, maces, and leashes those great-mighty demons have created torrents of arrows, missiles, 
trees and boulders, and inundated Rama in that war. [3-25-33, 34a]

verses.sanskrit: द्रुम वर्षाणि मुंचन्तः शिला वर्षाणि राक्षसाः ।तद् बभूव अद्भुतम् युद्धम् तुमुलम् रोम हर्षणम् ॥३-२५-३४॥
verses.text:
Again there chanced a highly gruesome war between Rama and those demons that is tumultuous, stunning, 
and a hair-raising. [3-25-34b, 35a]

verses.sanskrit: रामस्य अस्य महाघोरम् पुनः तेषाम् च रक्षसाम् ।ते समन्तात् अभिक्रुद्धा राघवम् पुनर् आर्दयन् ॥३-२५-३५॥
verses.text:
Those demons have resurfaced from all around pressurising Raghava in high dudgeon. On seeing the reappearance 
of demons from everywhere, with whom all the directions and inter-directions are fully filled, and who 
are storming torrents of arrows, then that exceptionally forceful Rama made a thunderous sound and launched 
an extremely blazing missile among the gather of demons, called gandharva missile. [3-25-35b, 36, 37]

verses.sanskrit: ततः सर्वा दिशो दृष्ट्वा प्रदिशाः च समावृताः ।राक्षसैः सर्वतः प्राप्तैः शर वर्षाभिः आवृतः ॥३-२५-३६॥
स कृत्वा भैरवम् नादम् अस्त्रम् परम भास्वरम् ।समयोजयत् गान्धर्वम् राक्षसेषु महाबलः ॥३-२५-३७॥
ततः शर सहस्राणि निर्ययुः चाप मण्डलात् ।सर्वा दश दिशो बानैः आपूर्यन्त समागतैः ॥३-२५-३८॥
verses.text:
Then from the nimbus of his bow, arrows in thousand have scurried out, and with them thus arrived all 
the ten sides are cramped up. [3-25-38]

verses.sanskrit: न आददानाम् शरान् घोरान् विमुंचंतम् शर उत्तमान् ।विकर्षमाणम् पश्यन्ति राक्षसाः ते शर आर्दिताः ॥३-२५-३९॥
verses.text:
Either the drawing of those supreme arrows from the quiver, or the traction of bowstring on placing 
them, or unloosing them from the bow is unperceived by those demons that are harrowing under those arrows. 
[3-25-39]

verses.sanskrit: शर अन्धकारम् आकाशम् आवृणोत्  स दिवाकरम् ।बभूव अवस्थितो रामः प्रक्षिपन् इव तान् शरान् ॥३-२५-४०॥
verses.text:
Darkness caused by the thick of the arrows overspread the sky including its sun, while Rama happened 
to persevere spewing forth his arrows. [3-25-40]

verses.sanskrit: युगपत् पतमानैः च युगपच्च हतैः भ्रिशम् ।युगपत् पतितैः चैव विकीर्णा वसुधा अभवत् ॥३-२५-४१॥
verses.text:
Far and wide the earth is scattered with herds that are falling instantaneously, hosts that have fallen 
instantly, and huddles that are already felled with an instantaneity. [3-25-41]

verses.sanskrit: निहताः पतिताः क्षीणा च्छिन्न भिन्न विदारिताः ।तत्र तत्र स्म दृश्यन्ते राक्षसाः ते सहस्रशः ॥३-२५-४२॥
verses.text:
Thousands of demons are noticeable hither and yon, killed, fallen and enfeebled, and slashed, gashed 
and ripped. [3-25-42]

verses.sanskrit: स उष्णीषैः उत्तम अन्गैः च स अंगदैः बाहुभिः तथा ।ऊरुभिः बाहुभिः च्छिन्नैः नाना रूपैः विभूषणैः ॥३-२५-४३॥
verses.text:
Some severed heads still have their headgears and some bare, some arms are with armlets and some bare, 
while some with their arms cut off, some with their thighs cut off, some with their divers patterns 
of ornaments on their bodies have fallen on ground. Numerous horses and elephants are felled. Chariots, 
royal-fans, royal-parasols, war-flags of very many kinds are ravaged in many ways. Hit down by Rama's 
arrows spears and tridents are variously splintered, swords broken to pieces, darts and hatchets shattered 
and strewn around. Also the boulders are pulverised, various amazing arrows have gone to rack and ruin 
in many ways. Spread with such wreckage the earth has become horrifying for a glance. [3-25-43, 44, 
45, 46]

verses.sanskrit: हयैः च द्विप मुख्यैः च रथैः भिन्नैः अनेकशः ।चामर व्यजनैः छत्रैः ध्वजैः नाना विधैः अपि ॥३-२५-४४॥
रामेण बाण अभिहतैः विच्छिन्नैः शूल पट्टिशैः ।खड्गैः खण्डीकृतैः प्रासैः विकीर्णैः च पश्वधैः ॥३-२५-४५॥
चूणिताभिः शिलाभिः च शरैः चित्रैः अनेकशः ।विच्छिन्नैः समरे भूमिः विस्तीर्णा आभूत् भयंकरा ॥३-२५-४६॥
तान् दृष्ट्वा निहतान् सर्वे रक्षसाः परम आतुराः ।न तत्र चलितुम् शक्ता रामम् पर पुरंजयम् ॥३-२५-४७॥
verses.text:
On seeing the killed demons all the surviving demons have become enervated and they are rendered incapable 
to make a move towards Rama, the conqueror of enemy's capitals. [3-25-47]
